seoTitle: St Marys vs Stanley — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between St Marys and Stanley. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: St Marys vs Stanley: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in St Marys and Stanley.
heading: St Marys vs Stanley — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between St Marys, Tasmania and Stanley, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: St Marys has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Stanley (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is St Marys or Stanley safer from flooding?
faq.answer: St Marys has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Stanley scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: St Marys scores 22/100 for bushfire risk. Stanley scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in St Marys or Stanley?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. St Marys has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
